properties.remark: HAIL MEASURED WITH A RULER AT GOLF BALL SIZE ON TAGHEE LN SOUTH OF W SIPHON RD. ALSO NUMEROUS STONES THE SIZE OF PING PONG BALLS. TIME ESTIMATED BASED ON RADAR.

HAIL FALLING AT THE INTERSECTION OF W SIPHON RD AND WEAVER RD. BEGAN AS DIME AND NICKEL SIZE AT 435 PM MDT, INCREASING TO QUARTER SIZE BETWEEN 436 PM AND 439 PM, REACHING HALF DOLLAR AND PING PONG BALL SIZE AT 441 PM. MEASURED WITH A RULER. HAIL STARTING TO COVER THE GROUND.
QUARTER SIZE HAIL STILL LAYING ON THE GROUND AT 510 PM MDT ON TANK FARM RD NORTH OF ROWLAND RD. TIME HAIL ORIGINALLY FELL ESTIMATED BASED ON RADAR AND SURROUNDING REPORTS.
NEAR FOOTHILL BLVD AND TRAIL CREEK RD.
MEASURED AT THE INTERSECTION OF POCATELLO CREEK RD AND BOOTH DR AFTER SOME MELTING OCCURRED. TIME OF EVENT ESTIMATED BASED ON RADAR.
MULTIPLE PUBLIC REPORTS AND PHOTOS OF QUARTER TO HALF DOLLAR SIZE HAIL RECEIVED ACROSS POCATELLO FROM 451 PM MDT TO 457 PM MDT. LIGHT HAIL ACCUMULATION NOTED ON THE GROUND AT SOME LOCATIONS.
1/2 INCH HAIL BEGAN AT 445 PM MDT, AND QUARTER SIZE HAIL FELL FROM 452 PM MDT TO 500 PM MDT ON GARY ST. LIGHT ACCUMULATION OF HAIL ON THE GROUND AND ROOFS.
EAST BENCH NEAR HOSPITAL.
LARGE TREE TOPPLED IN A CEMETERY. SOURCE OF PICTURES FROM SOCIAL MEDIA. TIME IS ESTIMATED BASED ON TIME OF WIND GUST.
ITD CLOSED THE ENTIRE STRETCH OF ID-32 FROM ASHTON TO TETONIA DUE TO BLOWING DUST AND LOW VISIBILITY. ALSO CONFIRMED BY COUNTY EM AND SPOTTER.
MEASURED AT MATERIALS FUELS COMPLEX NOAA ARL MESONET SITE. TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.
MEASURED AT THE ROVER NOAA ARL MESONET SITE. TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.
MEASURED AT THE TERRETON NOAA ARL MESONET SITE. TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.
MEASURED AT THE NOAA ARL MESONET SITE NEAR MINIDOKA.

MULTIPLE TREES AND TREE LIMBS DOWN IN SHOSHONE. ROOF RIPPED OFF A BARN. POWER LINES DOWN. 100 PEOPLE WITHOUT POWER IN SHOSHONE, AND 400 PEOPLE WITHOUT POWER IN DIETRICH. REPORTS FROM KMVT NEWS AND THE PUBLIC. TIME ESTIMATED BASED ON RADAR.
MULTIPLE TREES UPROOTED IN BELLEVUE. LARGEST TREE WAS APPROXIMATELY 2 FEET IN DIAMETER. TIME ESTIMATED BASED ON RADAR.
69 MPH WIND GUST ON BALD MOUNTAIN
PHOTO OF QUARTER SIZE HAIL IN GOSHEN SHARED BY IDAHO FALLS MAGAZINE FACEBOOK PAGE. TIME ESTIMATED BASED ON RADAR.

PHOTO OF QUARTER SIZE TO SLIGHTLY LARGER THAN HALF DOLLAR SIZE HAIL SHARED ON THE EAST IDAHO NEWS FACEBOOK PAGE. MULTIPLE REPORTS OF AT LEAST QUARTER SIZE HAIL FROM THIS AREA. TIME ESTIMATED BASED ON RADAR.
TIME ESTIMATED BASED ON RADAR. SHARED ON EAST IDAHO NEWS FACEBOOK PAGE.
REPORTED AT SMITH HONDA. SHARED VIA SOCIAL MEDIA WITH KIFI LOCAL NEWS 8. TIME ESTIMATED BASED ON RADAR.
REPORTED ON LANDBANK STREET. TIME ESTIMATED BASED ON RADAR. PHOTO SHARED BY THE PUBLIC ON THE EAST IDAHO NEWS FACEBOOK PAGE.
NUMEROUS PUBLIC REPORTS OF NICKEL, QUARTER, AND HALF DOLLAR SIZE HAIL IN IDAHO FALLS, ESPECIALLY ACROSS THE WESTERN HALF OF THE CITY. HAIL LIGHTLY COVERED THE GROUND IN SOME AREAS.
PING PONG BALL SIZE HAIL REPORTED ON THE WEST SIDE OF IDAHO FALLS. TIME ESTIMATED BASED ON RADAR.
REPORTED AT THE ARL FRD OFFICE IN IDAHO FALLS. TIME ESTIMATED BASED ON RADAR.
TIME ESTIMATED BASED ON RADAR. SHARED WITH EAST IDAHO NEWS ON FACEBOOK.

HAIL SLIGHTLY SMALLER THAN GOLF BALL SIZE REPORTED JUST NORTH OF ABERDEEN VIA SOCIAL MEDIA. TIME ESTIMATED BASED ON RADAR.
HAIL SLIGHTLY SMALLER THAN GOLF BALL SIZE REPORTED JUST NORTH OF ABERDEEN VIA SOCIAL MEDIA. VEHICLES DENTED AND PLASTIC VEHICLE TRIM BROKEN. GUTTER ON HOME DAMAGED. TIME ESTIMATED BASED ON RADAR.
REPORT OF HAIL 3/4 INCH TO 1 INCH.

DELAYED REPORT. APPROXIMATELY 10 TREES DOWN AND LARGE TREE LIMBS DOWN AT DOWNATA HOT SPRINGS. FALLING TREES AND LIMBS KNOCKED DOWN SEVERAL FENCES. LARGE WATER SLIDE DAMAGED AND CLOSED. MINOR DAMAGE TO RV TRAILERS. INFORMATION OBTAINED FROM KPVI NEWS AND SOCIAL MEDIA POSTS. TIME ESTIMATED BASED ON NEWS REPORTS AND RADAR.
HIGHWAY 89 COMPLETELY COVERED WITH HAIL, WITH NUMEROUS STONES TO SLIGHTLY LARGER THAN QUARTER SIZE. TRAVEL IS VERY SLIPPERY AND HAZARDOUS. LITTLE TO NO WIND WITH STORM AND NO WIND DAMAGE OBSERVED.
MESONET STATION MOFI1, MOBURG CANYON.

MULTIPLE PHOTOS AND VIDEOS OF HAIL MEASURED TO AT LEAST QUARTER SIZE IN PRESTON. PRESTON CITIZEN NEWSPAPER REPORTS WINDOW BROKEN OUT OF A CAR BY THE HAIL ON SOUTH 1ST STREET EAST. HAIL COVERED THE GROUND IN SOME AREAS. TIME ESTIMATED BASED ON RADAR.
WATER OVER BUCKBOARD LANE. PHOTO AND REPORT SHARED ON KIFI LOCAL NEWS 8 FACEBOOK PAGE.
SEVERAL INCHES OF WATER REPORTED ON CITY STREETS ON THE WEST SIDE OF IDAHO FALLS.
MULTIPLE REPORTS AND PHOTOS FROM SOCIAL MEDIA AND LOCAL NEWS OUTLETS OF STREET FLOODING IN IDAHO FALLS. WATER ALSO REPORTED IN SEVERAL BASEMENTS. UNDERPASS FLOODED ON YELLOWSTONE AVE.
SEVERAL STREETS FLOODED NEAR GRANDVIEW DRIVE AND SATURN AVE WITH WATER OVER THE CURBS AND INTO YARDS. WATER ENTERING ONE BASEMENT. REPORTS SHARED ON EAST IDAHO NEWS FACEBOOK PAGE.
ROAD FLOODED NEAR RUSS FREEMAN PARK.
VEHICLE STUCK IN 2 FEET OF WATER ON 17TH STREET. REPORT SHARED ON EAST IDAHO NEWS FACEBOOK PAGE.
FLOODING AT LEAST SEVERAL INCHES DEEP AT THE INTERSECTION OF NORTH BLVD AND SHELLEY STREET. REPORTED BY KIFI LOCAL NEWS 8.
RAWS MESONET STATION TR256. THUNDERSTORM OUTFLOW WINDS.

IDAHO DEPARTMENT OF TRANSPORTATION REPORTS INTERSTATE 15 IS CLOSED BETWEEN EXITS 118 AND 143 DUE TO LOW VISIBILITY IN BLOWING DUST. AT LEAST ONE THREE CAR ACCIDENT WAS REPORTED AS A RESULT OF LOW VISIBILITY.

UPDATED...INTERSTATE 15 NORTHBOUND AND SOUTHBOUND CLOSED FROM EXIT 118/BROADWAY STREET IN IDAHO FALLS NORTH TO EXIT 143/HIGHWAY 33 BETWEEN ROBERTS AND HAMER DUE TO BLOWING DUST AND SIGNIFICANTLY REDUCED VISIBILITIES. SECTION REOPENED AT APPROXIMATELY 704 PM.
INTERSTATE 15 CLOSED NEAR ROBERTS FROM BLOWING DUST AND REDUCED VISIBILITIES.
MULTIPLE REPORTS VIA BROADCAST MEDIA OF A TORNADO BETWEEN APPROXIMATELY 225PM AND 235PM ON THE BENCH BETWEEN IDAHO FALLS AND RIRIE. PRELIMINARY RATING OF EF0.
